steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: “The Limón technique... is known for its emphasis on breath control and its interplay of weight and weightlessness” is an independent clause.

After the blank: “dancers may explore, for example, the moment of mid-air suspension at the top of a jump” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for connecting two independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two independent clauses that express related ideas.

In this context, the second clause exemplifies the technique's interplay of weight and weightlessness.

Option D correctly uses a semicolon to connect the two clauses.

Option A omits punctuation (run-on), Option B uses “which” (creating grammatical confusion), and Option C uses only a comma (comma splice).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
